q: The SI unit of capacitance is the _______.
A _______ is a circuit component that stores electrical energy.
The capacitor was originally known as a _______.
A capacitor stores electrical energy in the form of an _______ field.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is _______ proportional to the area of the plates.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is _______ proportional to the distance between the plates.
An _______ is a material that does not conduct electricity.
The insulating material between the plates of a capacitor is called a _______.
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors in _______ is Cp = C1 + C2 + C3 + ...
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors in _______ is 1/Cs = 1/C1 + 1/C2 + 1/C3 + ...
The energy stored in a capacitor is U = _______.
Electrolytic capacitors use the _______ layer as the dielectric.
The capacitance of the _______ capacitor can be mechanically adjusted.
The unit of electric potential is the _______.
Electric field strength is a _______ quantity.
The potential at a point is a _______ quantity.
Coulomb's law states that the force between two point charges is inversely proportional to the _______ of the distance between them.
The _______ of a conductor is the ratio of the charge on it to its potential.
One farad is equal to one _______ per volt.
_______ capacitors use thin sheets of metal foil as electrodes.
The capacitance values of _______ capacitors are stable with respect to the applied voltage.
The failure of the _______ capacitors can cause an explosion or a fire.
In _______ capacitors, air is often used as a dielectric.
The electric field inside a charged _______ is zero.
The surface charge density is denoted by the Greek letter _______.
_______ is the charge per unit area of a surface.
The potential difference between two points is the _______ done in moving a unit positive charge.
Electric field lines start from _______ charges and end on negative charges.
The ratio of the capacitance with a dielectric to the capacitance with a vacuum is called the _______.
Capacitors in parallel all have the same _______.
Capacitors in series all have the same _______.
Mica capacitors are often used in _______ circuits.
The breakdown of a dielectric is called _______.
A _______ is used to store large amounts of charge at a relatively low potential.
The unit of electric field strength is _______ per metre.
Coulomb's law is valid for _______ point charges.
The work done to charge a capacitor is stored as _______ energy.
The _______ constant of vacuum is 1.
Like charges _______ and unlike charges attract.
The electric potential at an _______ distance is zero.
Surface charge density is highest at _______ points of a conductor.
An electric _______ consists of two equal and opposite charges separated by a distance.
The capacitance C is equal to _______ / V.
_______ capacitors use plastic film such as polyester or polycarbonate.
The total charge of capacitors in parallel is the _______ of the individual charges.
The reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance in series is the sum of the _______ of individual capacitances.
The value of 1/4πε₀ is approximately _______ N m^2 C^-2.
A _______ is an insulating material that can be polarized by an electric field.
Work must be done to _______ a capacitor.
The _______ of the capacitor depends on the geometry of the conductors.
